problem: ZIP metadata was nondeterministic and source traversal followed symlinks.
mathematical_or_language_reason: ZIP bytes depend on metadata; is_file follows symlinks.
failure_example: Two builds can hash differently or include an external target.
fix: Fixed metadata, regular-file bounds, atomic build, external sidecar and double-build validation.

problem: User-controlled --target could be recursively deleted by --force or --uninstall.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Destruction lacked ownership and boundary invariants.
failure_example: Uninstall an arbitrary important directory.
fix: Managed marker, dangerous-target rejection, staging, unique backup and rollback.

problem: Second-resolution project IDs could collide and initialization was non-atomic.
mathematical_or_language_reason: A one-second timestamp is not injective.
failure_example: Two projects initialized in one second share an ID.
fix: UTC plus UUID ID and staged replacement.

problem: Claim–Evidence references were only checked in one direction.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Graph consistency requires both directions.
failure_example: CL-1 lists EV-1 while EV-1 omits CL-1.
fix: Bidirectional links, duplicate checks and support/refute disjointness.

problem: Substring matching, duplicate keywords and reverse lexical ties produced unstable routing.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Scores changed under duplicate rules and ties ignored declaration order.
failure_example: plot matched subplotter.
fix: NFKC/casefold, word boundaries, deduplication, bounds, cache and stable ties.

problem: Mutable Action tags and narrow CI omitted major gates.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Tags are not content-addressed and one platform is not cross-platform evidence.
failure_example: actions/checkout@v4 can change without a repository diff.
fix: Pinned SHAs, read-only permissions, timeouts, 3-OS/4-Python matrix and quality gates.

problem: NaN/Infinity and non-atomic writes were accepted.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Non-finite values are outside standard JSON and partial writes violate atomicity.
failure_example: NaN state or truncated JSON.
fix: Finite JSON, strict UTF-8, bounds, fsync/replace and symlink guards.

problem: No bounded safe ZIP intake existed.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Member names are attacker-controlled paths.
failure_example: ../escape, absolute paths, links and compression bombs.
risk: Zip Slip and denial of service.
fix: Bounded validation and atomic manual extraction.

problem: Temporal, approval and idempotency invariants were incomplete.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Schema membership alone is not a state-machine proof.
failure_example: accepted without approval or updated_at before created_at.
fix: Transition, timestamp, rationale and approval gates.

problem: Schemas lacked stable IDs and a capability contract.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Unidentified open contracts permit drift.
failure_example: Duplicate IDs or extra fields accepted.
fix: Eight identified closed Draft 2020-12 schemas.
rollback: Version schemas instead of loosening silently.

problem: Tests were mainly positive existence checks.
mathematical_or_language_reason: They did not distinguish correct code from critical mutants.
failure_example: Installer only asserted SKILL.md exists.
fix: Negative, property, adversarial, deterministic and mutation tests.

problem: Child processes had no timeout or explicit shell policy.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Unbounded execution violates termination assumptions.
failure_example: Hung validation occupies a runner indefinitely.
fix: Argument vectors, shell=false, controlled environment and timeouts.
rollback: Raise timeout rather than remove it.

problem: errors=ignore and weak workflow scanning could hide opaque source.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Ignoring undecodable bytes makes scanning non-total.
failure_example: Encoded self-pushing workflow escapes audit.
fix: Strict inventory, symlink, pinned Action, bootstrap, secret and shell checks.

problem: Dependencies lacked upper bounds and quality tools.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Future major resolution is not reproducible.
failure_example: pytest>=8 resolves to an incompatible major.
fix: Compatible upper bounds and explicit tooling.

problem: The structure validator searched for a literal placeholder marker that also appeared in its own source, so it rejected the repository containing the validator.
mathematical_or_language_reason: A detector whose literal signature is included in its own scanned domain is self-referential and cannot distinguish the detector from a target occurrence.
failure_example: python scripts/validate_structure.py reported placeholder in scripts/validate_structure.py on the exact remote snapshot.
risk: A valid repository fails validation and CI signals become untrustworthy.
fix: Construct the marker from adjacent string fragments at runtime so the scanner source does not contain the target token.
compatibility_impact: Only the false self-match is removed; real placeholder tokens still fail.
rollback: Revert only with an equivalent self-exclusion mechanism.

problem: The capability-filter fixture used a category label that does not exist in the catalog.
mathematical_or_language_reason: A fixture outside the production domain tests an empty-set accident rather than the intended category-selection invariant.
failure_example: The test requested 数据、统计与可视化 while the catalog uses 数据统计与可视化, producing no records.
risk: A correct capability loader appears broken, and later weakening of the assertion could create a false green.
fix: Use the canonical catalog category and assert both non-empty output and exact category equality.
rollback: Do not restore a fixture value absent from the catalog.

problem: Tests and production modules relied on mutable sys.path ordering and bare sibling imports.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Import success depended on prior process state, violating test independence and referential consistency of module resolution.
failure_example: tests/test_capabilities.py failed alone with ModuleNotFoundError: common but could pass after another test inserted scripts into sys.path.
risk: Full-suite green can hide isolated failures; behavior depends on collection order.
fix: Make scripts an explicit package, use scripts.* imports, remove test sys.path mutation, and verify every module in a fresh interpreter.
compatibility_impact: Direct CLI execution remains supported through an explicit repository-root bootstrap.
rollback: Revert package and test import changes together only after equivalent isolation tests exist.

problem: CI formatted, linted, and type-checked only selected files, allowing defects in unlisted scripts and tests to remain invisible.
mathematical_or_language_reason: A partial gate cannot establish a repository-wide invariant when its membership list is manually incomplete.
failure_example: Strict mypy over all scripts exposed six errors that the previous ten-module command never evaluated.
risk: Static-quality claims overstate the checked surface.
fix: Run Ruff format/lint across scripts and tests and strict mypy across all scripts; audit the workflow for required gate markers.
compatibility_impact: New files are automatically included and may reveal real failures.
rollback: Do not return to a hand-maintained partial file list.

problem: Every capability query reread the index and all shard files from disk.
mathematical_or_language_reason: Repeated immutable catalog parsing makes I/O proportional to query count instead of catalog revisions.
failure_example: One hundred identical loads performed repeated shard reads before caching.
risk: Avoidable latency and I/O amplification in repeated searches.
fix: Cache the immutable baseline by resolved index path and return deep copies so callers cannot mutate cached records.
compatibility_impact: Catalog changes within one long-lived process require cache invalidation or process restart.
rollback: Retain the copy-on-return isolation if cache strategy changes.

problem: The mutation smoke covered only three changes and did not first prove that the unmodified baseline tests passed in the isolated copied repository.
mathematical_or_language_reason: A mutant is only killed when a previously passing test becomes failing; a pre-existing failure is not mutation evidence.
failure_example: A broken baseline could be counted as a killed mutant without causal attribution.
risk: Inflated mutation score and false confidence in safety invariants.
fix: Validate the copied baseline first, expand to fifteen named critical mutants, and emit machine-readable per-mutant evidence.
compatibility_impact: Mutation CI takes longer but remains bounded.
rollback: Never remove baseline validation when changing mutation tooling.

problem: The suite had no automatic guard for process-global state and no reverse, seeded-random, repeated, or per-module execution gates.
mathematical_or_language_reason: A single collection order samples only one permutation and cannot disprove order-dependent coupling.
failure_example: The bare-import defect passed only after another module polluted sys.path.
risk: Nondeterministic failures and false full-suite green results.
fix: Add an autouse state guard, fresh-process import tests, independent module jobs, reverse order, recorded random seed, and five consecutive full-suite runs.
compatibility_impact: Tests that intentionally mutate globals must restore them before teardown.
rollback: Narrow only precisely documented pytest-internal allowances; do not disable the guard wholesale.
